title: Preparation of Anhydrous Halides of Lanthanides.. Part I : Preparation of Anhydrous Halides of Lanthanum and Cerium (III) H. C. Pant, M. g. Bain' and I. B. Ghobs
author: H. C. Paul
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
A  brief but critical review elite method of preparation of lanthanum and cerium (III) halides is given. 
Anhydrous halides of these metals have been prepared by a new method in which suspension of the respective acetate in toluene or benzene is successively saturated with dry ammonia and anhydrous halogen hydroid gas followed by isolation of The complex ammonium lanthanide h ali de and sublimation of ammonium halide therefrom. The yields are almost quantitative. However, pure iodides could not be prepared by this method.
